mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity returns as recession fears dominate pricing.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE spikes above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising real borrowing costs, causing immediate negative short-term equity repricing.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading exceeding 325.0 signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces equity markets to discount future earnings growth, producing negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL breaches the 325.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day SPX direction across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to aggregate output, triggering immediate negative short-term equity adjustments.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric fails to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in the industrial production index during a post-shock recovery regime confirms supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, generating positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases in recovery regimes are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 65% of observed periods.
